spec_match.rationale: The page now teaches the CSS Conditional Level 5 identifier syntax, including named-feature(anchor-position-follows-transforms) and named-feature(single-axis-scroll-container), while preserving a quoted-string preset as an explicit invalid case.

interactive.rationale: The method tabs, custom input, presets, Enter key, generated code, and batch table all run live CSS.supports() evaluations without inline event handlers.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The concept matches the feature index by showing the JavaScript CSSOM form of the same named-feature() support condition.

edge_cases.rationale: The presets cover registered identifiers, an unknown identifier that must return false, selector and property conditions, and the invalid quoted-string form; Chrome 148 confirmed both CSS.supports('display: grid') and CSS.supports('(display: grid)') evaluate true.

references.rationale: The page links to the ChromeStatus entry and the CSS Conditional Level 5 named-feature() grammar section.

design_system.rationale: The touched UI uses showcase CSS variables, explicit button text/background colors, helper classes instead of inline styles, and stable tab/preset controls.

summary: Repaired after chrome-devtools-mcp reproduced the old default as named-feature("anchor-position-follows-transforms") returning false in Chrome 148. The concept now defaults to identifier syntax, keeps the quoted form only as an invalid preset, adds the second registered name, and closes the stale property-parentheses and ChromeStatus-link questions.
